context: Tests whether the agent correctly designs the reorder configuration and stock alert data models with all required fields, computes available inventory correctly, applies the right alert type logic, and implements deduplication to prevent re-firing resolved alerts.

checklist.name: reorder_configs fields
checklist.description: reorder_configs model/table includes ALL of: variant_id (or variantId), location_id (or locationId), reorder_point (or reorderPoint), reorder_quantity (or reorderQuantity), supplier_id (or supplierId), lead_time_days (or leadTimeDays), use_dynamic_reorder_point (or useDynamicReorderPoint), created_at (or createdAt)

checklist.name: stock_alerts fields
checklist.description: stock_alerts model/table includes ALL of: variant_id, location_id, alert_type, triggered_at, available_at_trigger, reorder_point_at_trigger, resolved_at, notification_sent, acknowledged_by

checklist.name: alert_type enum
checklist.description: alert_type field accepts exactly three values: 'low_stock', 'out_of_stock', and 'overstock' (or equivalent enum)

checklist.name: available calculation
checklist.description: Available stock is computed as onHand minus reserved (e.g. level.onHand - level.reserved or on_hand - reserved), NOT just using onHand alone

checklist.name: out_of_stock condition
checklist.description: alert_type is set to 'out_of_stock' specifically when available equals 0 (not just <= reorderPoint)

checklist.name: low_stock condition
checklist.description: alert_type is set to 'low_stock' when available is greater than 0 AND less than or equal to the reorder point

checklist.name: deduplication check
checklist.description: Before creating a new alert, the code queries for an existing unresolved alert for the same variantId + locationId + alertType combination (where resolvedAt is null)

checklist.name: resolvedAt guard
checklist.description: The deduplication check specifically filters on resolvedAt being null (not just any existing alert), so resolved alerts do not block new ones

checklist.name: dynamic reorder support
checklist.description: The job code checks use_dynamic_reorder_point / useDynamicReorderPoint flag and computes a different reorder point when it is true

checklist.name: 20% safety buffer
checklist.description: When computing the dynamic reorder point in the job, the formula uses a 1.2 multiplier (20% safety stock buffer): Math.ceil(velocity * leadTimeDays * 1.2) or equivalent
